title: TechScreen verified ID-token claims (IdTokenClaims, T07)
description: The subset of Identity Platform (GCIP) ID-token claims the TechScreen backend consumes AFTER RS256 signature verification against Google's securetoken certs. Producers: GCIP mints the standard claims; the auth-claims blocking function (infra/functions/auth_claims/) injects `role` and `hd` from configs/auth-roles.yaml. Consumer: app/backend/services/auth.py (IdTokenVerifier) enforces every property below and maps failures to HTTP 401 — except an absent/non-staff `role`, which maps to HTTP 403 (valid identity, no authorization). See specs/021-t07-identity-sso/data-model.md and ADR-024.

iss.description: Token issuer. Must equal https://securetoken.google.com/<GCP_PROJECT> for the backend's configured project (tech-screen-493720 in both environments — the Identity Platform config is project-global).

aud.description: Token audience. GCIP sets this to the GCP project ID; must equal the backend's GCP_PROJECT setting.

sub.description: Stable GCIP user ID (uid) of the signed-in staff member. Non-empty; surfaced as request.state.user['sub'] and the future audit actor reference.

email.description: Workspace account email. Defense in depth: the backend additionally requires the domain suffix to match AUTH_ALLOWED_DOMAIN even though `hd` is also enforced. Staff email is PII-adjacent config: it flows into request.state.user for audit use and must never be logged (constitution §15).

email_verified.description: Must be literally true. Google-provider sign-ins arrive verified; anything else is rejected with 401.

hd.description: Hosted (Workspace) domain. NOT copied from Google's IdP token by GCIP — injected by the auth-claims blocking function for admitted accounts (research R4). Must equal the backend's AUTH_ALLOWED_DOMAIN (n-ix.com at MVP).

role.description: TechScreen staff role, injected by the auth-claims blocking function from configs/auth-roles.yaml (constitution §16). Feeds Principal.role and the require_roles gate. A token without this claim is a valid identity with no authorization: HTTP 403 with a body naming configs/auth-roles.yaml.

iat.description: Issued-at, unix seconds. Must not be in the future (verified by the JWT decode step).

exp.description: Expiry, unix seconds — GCIP tokens live at most one hour; exp > iat by construction. Must not be in the past (verified by the JWT decode step). Token revocation is not checked at MVP (ADR-024): removing a role takes effect at the next sign-in.
